title: Fleischner 2017 — solid nodule, single, < 6 mm
text: Low-risk patient: no routine follow-up. High-risk patient: optional CT at 12 months. Note 1: 'Low risk' = minimal/absent smoking history; 'High risk' = age 50+ heavy smoking or asbestos/radon exposure. Note 2: Lower threshold for follow-up if upper-lobe location, irregular margins, or suspicious morphology.

title: Fleischner 2017 — solid nodule, single, 6-8 mm
text: Low-risk: CT at 6-12 months; consider CT at 18-24 months. High-risk: CT at 6-12 months; CT at 18-24 months. Note: Initial follow-up at 3 months if very suspicious morphology.

title: Fleischner 2017 — solid nodule, single, > 8 mm
text: All risk groups: consider CT at 3 months OR PET/CT (functional imaging) OR tissue sampling. Choice depends on size, morphology, comorbidity, patient preference.

title: Fleischner 2017 — sub-solid nodule, single, < 6 mm
text: Ground-glass nodule (GGN): no routine follow-up. Part-solid: no routine follow-up. Note: In select patients with high risk and suspicious morphology, follow-up at 2 and 4 years may be considered.

title: Fleischner 2017 — pure ground-glass nodule, single, >= 6 mm
text: CT at 6-12 months to confirm persistence, then CT every 2 years until 5 years. If GGN resolves or decreases in size at 6-12 months, no further follow-up. If stable, continue surveillance.

title: Fleischner 2017 — part-solid nodule, single, >= 6 mm
text: CT at 3-6 months to confirm persistence. If solid component remains < 6 mm, annual CT for 5 years. If solid component grows or >= 6 mm, consider resection, PET/CT, or tissue sampling. Note: Part-solid nodules with solid components >= 6 mm have higher likelihood of malignancy.

title: Fleischner 2017 — multiple nodules, < 6 mm
text: Low-risk: no routine follow-up. High-risk: optional CT at 12 months. Manage based on most-suspicious nodule.

title: Fleischner 2017 — multiple nodules, >= 6 mm
text: All risk: CT at 3-6 months, then consider at 18-24 months. Manage based on most-suspicious nodule (typically dominant or upper-lobe).

title: Fleischner 2017 — patients to whom guidelines do NOT apply
text: Patients under age 35 (low risk of primary lung cancer); patients with known primary cancer (use cancer-specific surveillance); patients undergoing lung cancer screening per Lung-RADS; immunocompromised patients (infection-driven nodules).
